steps.title: Use complementary angles
steps.content: For acute angles, the sine of one angle equals the cosine of its complement. Since $\cos(Q)=\sin(R)$, angles $Q$ and $R$ are complementary.

steps.title: Set up the angle sum
steps.content:
Complementary angles have a sum of $90^\circ$. Use the given angle measures.
$$(x+54)+(4x+6)=90$$

steps.title: Solve for the value
steps.content:
Combine like terms and solve.
$$5x+60=90$$
$$5x=30$$
$$x=6$$
The answer is A.
